sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Atlético Madrid's last 5 matches show mixed results with 3 wins and 2 losses, scoring 7 goals but conceding 4. Elche, on the other hand, has shown resilience with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, scoring 6 goals and conceding 7. Atlético's recent competitive loss to Espanyol highlights some defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Atlético Madrid dominated with a 4-0 win in January 2025, while Elche managed a narrow 1-0 victory in May 2023. Historically, Atlético has the upper hand with 4 wins in the last 5 matchups against Elche.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atlético Madrid's season stats indicate a strong possession game (60% average) and high final third entries (rank 2), though their goal conversion is underwhelming (0% big chances converted). Elche struggles similarly in attack but shows decent defensive organization with fewer goals conceded per game compared to expectations.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Atlético's ability to create chances in the final third and their historical dominance over Elche could be decisive. Elche's recent form suggests they can hold their own, but their lower league position (10th vs. Atlético’s 17th) and weaker attacking stats may limit their threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The match between Atlético Madrid and Elche at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano is finely poised at 1-1 after an engaging first half. Both teams found the net early, with Atlético taking the lead through Alexander Sørloth in the 8th minute, only for Elche to respond quickly with a goal from Rafa Mir in the 15th minute. The game has been competitive, with near-equal ball possession and both sides showing moments of promise in attack. However, Atlético Madrid have looked more threatening overall, creating more chances and dominating in key attacking areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid started the game with strong intent, building early momentum with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. Elche had their moments of pushback, particularly around the time of their equalizer, showing brief but effective attacking phases. As the half progressed, Atlético regained control with several sustained periods of pressure, especially in the latter stages of the first half. Elche struggled to maintain consistent forward momentum, with their attacking efforts becoming more sporadic towards the break. This suggests Atlético have the upper hand in dictating the tempo of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid have shown superiority in several statistical areas at half-time. Their dominance in the final third is evident, with a high number of entries and penalty area penetrations compared to Elche. They’ve also created more significant scoring opportunities, although they missed a big chance to extend their lead. Elche, on the other hand, have been more conservative in attack, with fewer entries into dangerous areas and no big chances created. Defensively, Elche have picked up a couple of yellow cards, which could impact their approach in the second half, while Atlético have maintained discipline with no bookings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Alexander Sørloth has been the standout performer in the first half, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat in the box. His presence up front could be crucial in the second half. Dàvid Hancko also contributed with an assist and a strong defensive display, while Thiago Almada’s creativity in midfield has helped unlock Elche’s defense. On the Elche side, Rafa Mir’s goal highlights his danger as a focal point in attack, and he’ll likely remain their primary outlet for a potential upset. Álvaro Rodriguez provided the assist for Mir’s goal and could play a pivotal role in creating further chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid are likely to push for a win in the second half, leveraging their attacking depth and home advantage. Expect them to maintain high pressure in the final third and exploit Elche’s defensive vulnerabilities, especially given the away side’s limited penetration in dangerous areas. Elche may adopt a more defensive stance, looking to protect their point and counter when possible. However, their lack of possession wins in the final third suggests they might struggle to create consistent threats unless they adjust tactically. Atlético’s ability to convert their chances will likely be the deciding factor in this match.
